2606.25819.title: Beyond Function Calling: Benchmarking Tool-Using Agents under Tool-Environment Unreliability
2606.25819.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly deployed as agents that solve tasks by interacting with external tool environments. Although recent tool-use benchmarks increasingly cover complex task settings, they still largely assume clean, stable, and trustworthy tool environments, leaving tool-environment unreliability insufficiently examined. We introduce ToolBench-X, a benchmark for evaluating agents under recoverable reliability hazards. ToolBench-X contains executable multi-step tasks across diverse domains and sequential, parallel, and mixed workflows, each paired with deterministic tools and a canonical final answer for automatic evaluation. Starting from clean tool environments, ToolBench-X injects five structured hazard types: Specification Drift, Invocation Error, Execution Failure, Output Drift, and Cross-source Conflict. Crucially, each injected instance remains solvable through at least one valid recovery path, such as retrying, fallback, verification, or cross-checking. Experiments reveal a substantial reliability gap: agents that perform well with reliable tools often fail under recoverable hazards. Further analysis shows that failures are driven less by tool-use volume or inference budget than by limited hazard diagnosis and ineffective recovery. Targeted recovery hints recover many failed tasks, while test-time scaling yields more limited gains. These results suggest that tool-use evaluation should move beyond function-call accuracy toward task completion under unreliable tool environments. The code and data is available at https://github.com/Foreverskyou/ToolBench-X.
2606.25819.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用作通過與外部工具環境互動來解決任務的代理。儘管最近的工具使用基準越來越涵蓋複雜的任務設置，但它們仍然在很大程度上假設工具環境是乾淨、穩定且可信的，這使得工具環境的不可靠性未得到充分檢查。我們介紹了ToolBench-X，一個用於評估代理在可恢復可靠性危害下的基準。ToolBench-X包含跨多個領域的可執行多步任務，以及順序、並行和混合工作流程，每個任務都配有確定性工具和自動評估的標準最終答案。從乾淨的工具環境開始，ToolBench-X注入了五種結構化的危害類型：規範漂移、調用錯誤、執行失敗、輸出漂移和跨源衝突。關鍵是，每個注入的實例都可以通過至少一條有效的恢復路徑解決，例如重試、回退、驗證或交叉檢查。實驗顯示出顯著的可靠性差距：在可靠工具下表現良好的代理在可恢復危害下往往失敗。進一步分析顯示，失敗的驅動因素不在於工具使用量或推理預算，而在於有限的危害診斷和無效的恢復。針對性的恢復提示恢復了許多失敗的任務，而測試時的擴展則帶來了更有限的增益。這些結果表明，工具使用評估應該超越功能調用的準確性，朝著在不可靠工具環境下的任務完成邁進。代碼和數據可在 https://github.com/Foreverskyou/ToolBench-X 獲得。
2606.25819.author: Yang Tian et.al.
2606.25819.authors: Yang Tian, Zhengpeng Shi, Bo Zhao
